---
title: "Matthew Lavallee"
format:
  html:
    theme:
      light: [cosmo, theme-light.scss]
      dark: [cosmo, theme-dark.scss]
about:
  template: trestles
  image: files/headshot.jpg
  image-alt: "Headshot of Matthew Lavallee"
  links:
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:mlavall3@jhu.edu
    - icon: mortarboard
      text: Google Scholar
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=5OUEFSUAAAAJ&hl=en
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/matthewlavallee
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/mattylavallee/
    - text: "{{< fa brands bluesky >}} Bluesky"
      href: https://bsky.app/profile/mplavallee.com
comments: false
---

I am a PhD student in the [Department of Health Policy and Management](https://publichealth.jhu.edu/departments/health-policy-and-management) at the Johns Hopkins Bloomberg School of Public Health, specializing in health economics.

My research focuses on **Medicaid**, **social safety net programs**, and **their interactions**. I study how public insurance programs and other safety net policies shape program participation, access to care, and health outcomes.

## Education

{{< fa graduation-cap >}} **PhD in Health Economics**, Johns Hopkins Bloomberg School of Public Health *(expected 2027)*

{{< fa graduation-cap >}} **BA in Economics & Mathematics**, Hampshire College *(2016 - 2020)*
